key_insight: Strain rate and deformation length scale affect indentation plasticity in tungsten in different ways: rate mainly changes flow stress, while depth controls plastic-zone growth, pile-up, and GND buildup.
engineering_relevance: This separation enables more reliable constitutive models for neutron-damaged tungsten under transient loading, improving prediction of irradiation hardening and localized deformation.
method: Spherical nanoindentation on unirradiated and neutron-irradiated single-crystal tungsten across 3.2e-5 to 3.2e-3 s^-1
AFM and HR-EBSD measured pile-up, residual lattice strain, and GND distributions
Strain-gradient CPFE with thermally activated slip, GND hardening, irradiation obstacle hardening, and softening
evidence_signal: A CPFE model calibrated with one experimental condition reproduced the remaining strain-rate cases without further tuning and was also used to separate strain-rate and indentation-depth effects.
watch_item: The framework is calibrated on a specific tungsten indentation dataset, so transfer to other irradiation states, temperatures, or loading geometries should be revalidated.
paper_title: Decoupling Strain-Rate Sensitivity and Deformation Length Scale Effects in Neutron-Irradiated Tungsten: A Coupled Nano-Indentation, HR-EBSD and Crystal Plasticity Study
